methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Gallox Semiconductors is a Seed-stage semiconductor company that has raised $5M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public peers (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private companies in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, including Series A-stage firms such as Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two valuation methods proved applicable. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $5M total raised as a proxy for implicit valuation, applies a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from Seed to next-stage, yielding $12M. Peer-median anchoring across the four Series A comparables produces a median valuation of $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via price-to-sales multiples (median 25.3x across the sector) cannot be applied due to the absence of revenue disclosure from Gallox. The three public semiconductors show the sector's rich valuation multiples but provide no meaningful anchor without the target's revenue figure.

The triangulation of two applicable methods supports a valuation range of $11M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $756M. The wide dispersion reflects substantial uncertainty: the Stage-Progression method reflects minimal dilution risk but lacks market validation, while the Peer-Median method captures current market appetite for chip-design firms but assumes Gallox can reach peer-equivalent scale and market position. Near-term revenue traction and technical differentiation will be critical to narrowing this range.
